steps.title: Compare compound types
steps.content: The student wants to contrast dichloromethane and tetradecafluorohexane. The notes distinguish them by class and composition: dichloromethane is a hydrochlorocarbon with hydrogen, chlorine, and carbon, while tetradecafluorohexane is a perfluorocarbon with fluorine and carbon.

steps.title: Reject shared-feature choices
steps.content: B and C emphasize that both compounds are refrigerants used in cooling technologies, and D discusses only tetradecafluorohexane. The needed sentence sets the two compounds' different categories and elements side by side. The answer is A.
